title: AFRICOM Announces 56th Somalia Airstrike of 2026, Targeting ISIS-Somalia Near Bosaso
detail: U.S. Africa Command (AFRICOM) announced on April 19, 2026 (AFRICOM Press Release #36380) that it had conducted its 56th Somalia airstrike of the year on April 17, targeting ISIS-Somalia in Puntland approximately 30 miles (48 km) southeast of Bosaso. The strike continues AFRICOM's intensified 2026 campaign against ISIS-Somalia cave complexes in the Golis/Cal Miskaad mountain range, following previous strikes on March 8, 16, and 18 in the same area. At 56 strikes by April 17, AFRICOM is on pace to surpass 200 Somalia airstrikes in 2026 — more than triple the prior annual record of 63 (2019). The accelerated tempo reflects expanded strike authority granted to AFRICOM by Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth early in 2026. ISIS-Somalia operates in Puntland's Bari Region with a small but persistent presence that AFRICOM and the FGS have prioritized alongside the larger Al-Shabaab campaign in south-central Somalia.

media.caption: AFRICOM conducts 56th airstrike of 2026 against ISIS-Somalia approximately 30 miles southeast of Bosaso, Puntland — April 17, 2026

title: Al-Shabaab Seizes Adan Yabal; President Mohamud Orders Immediate SNA Offensive to Retake Town
detail: Al-Shabaab seized the strategic town of Adan Yabal (Aadan Yabaal) in Hiiraan Region in heavy clashes with Somali National Army forces around April 19, 2026. In response, President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud convened an emergency meeting at Villa Somalia with SNA Commander General Yusuf Odowaa Rage and senior military officers, ordering them to immediately launch new operations to reclaim Adan Yabal and other recently captured areas. Al-Shabaab fighters also reportedly killed 8 civilians including 5 women in the Adan Yabaal area — targeting business owners at a restaurant, military facility support operation, laundry facility, and garage. The fall of Adan Yabal is a significant setback in Hiiraan Region, the same area where the 2022 Macawisley-SNA offensive had made major gains. The seizure underscores Al-Shabaab's ability to exploit the governance vacuum created by Somalia's constitutional crisis: parliament's mandate expired April 14 without elections, and President Mohamud's own term expires May 15. Analysts note that Al-Shabaab historically accelerates offensive operations during periods of Somali political uncertainty.

media.caption: Al-Shabaab seizes Adan Yabal in Hiiraan Region; President Mohamud orders emergency SNA offensive to retake the town
